text:
Day four of theOlympicsis underway with 14 gold medal events on the agenda in Paris.

Team GB took their haul to eight medals on Mondaywith Tom Daley, Noah Williams and Tom Pidcock among those getting on the podium.

There is also more gold up for grabs for Team GB in swimming events with Lucy Renshall also chasing glory in judo following her early exit in Tokyo three years ago.

Charlotte Dujardin will have no role to playin the team and individual dressage events today with Becky Moody coming into replace her alongside Carl Hester and Charlotte Fry this afternoon.

Elsewhere,Simone Biles looked back to her imperious best on Sundayand will have the chance to claim her first medal of these Games in theartistic gymnastics final this evening.

Unfortunately,concerns remain over the triathlon as the event was again postponed due to concerns over pollution in the River Seine.

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

That’s the end of our live blog for the day – we’ll be back bright and early tomorrow morning! Here’s what’s on the menu for Wednesday…

Andy Murraydropped to his seat and sobbed as he andDan Evansreached theParisOlympics quarter-finals in another epic clash at Roland-Garros.

Full story and report here.

ð¥ Gold medal âð¥ Olympic record âð¬ð§ Great Britain’s Nathan Hales delivers an Olympic record of 48 out of 50 shots to take the gold medal in the trap final ð¯#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/o2scaLA3oY

Team GB added two gold medals to their overall tally today!

Nathan Hales clinched shooting gold in the men’s trap final. Great Britain also took gold in the men’s 4x200m freestyle relay.

Nada Hafez was seven months pregnant when she beat Elizabeth Tartakovsky 15-13 at the Paris Olympics, the Egyptian fencer has revealed.

Click here for the full story.

HOW IT FEELS! ð¥https://t.co/vPUSJWedhLpic.twitter.com/4MYGTuHPtL

Great Britain have claimed their first swimming gold of Paris 2024 but boy did they have to work for it.

James Guy made a superb start for the Brits in the first leg before Tom Dean was briefly pegged back by Germany and Australia.

But he reclaimed the lead towards the end of his leg and handed over to silver medallist Matt Richards who kept Great Britain’s noses in front.

Duncan Scott took over with a narrow lead and absolutely destroyed his rivals to ensure Great Britain defended their Olympic title in the men’s 4x200m freestyle relay.

Job done!

The Olympic dream LIVES ON for Dan Evans and Andy Murray! ð¬ð§ðThe@TeamGBduo are into the quarter-finals at#Paris2024.The show goes on for Murray!#BBCOlympics#Olympicspic.twitter.com/Dp0qz6Xnfp

‘Oh my goodness, absolutely jaw-dropping from Andy Murray and Dan Evans again,’ former British No.1 Annabel Croft told the BBC.

‘I literally cannot believe what we have seen. The drama that we have seen, the rollercoaster we have been on during that match, two hours and four minutes.

‘A slow burner that just got better and better and better. It just sums up Andy Murray and what he has done in his career. We are seeing quite a lot of emotion from him now they have shook hands’.

They’ve done it! Andy Murray and Dan Evans have secured victory!

The Team GB pair defeat Sander Gille and Joran Vliegen 6-3 6-7 (8-10) (11-9) to book their spot in the quarter-finals of the Olympic men’s doubles…

They will either next face Team USA’s Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul or the Dutch team of Robin Haase and Jean-Julien Rojer!

We’re going to a match tie break ð¬Can Andy Murray and Dan Evans pull it out the bag once again?#BBCOlympics#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/mqv8nRvrx8

Sander Gille and Joran Vliegen come out on top 10-8 in that second-set tie-break, so we’ve now gotanothertie-break to determine the match winners…

Brutal for the Team GB duo – who missed two match points there.

Andy Murray also kicked off at the umpire when a let was called out: ‘Mate, you have got to hear that, it is so obvious!’

After winning the first set 6-3, Andy Murray and Dan Evans are now set for a second-set tie-break here at Roland-Garros…

‘The Belgian pair have been bringing their level up and up and up during this match and they are beginning to become a bit of a problem,’ Annabel Croft told the BBC.

DANIEL WIFFEN! ð¤¯You are an OLYMPIC MEDALLIST and an OLYMPIC RECORD HOLDERð¥#TeamIreland|#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/tbLTFOV5i4

It’s always a gruelling race but the 23-year-old storms home in front to clinch Ireland’s first gold medal of the Paris Olympics.

He finishes the 800m in 7:38.19, finishing just ahead of America’s Bobby Finke. That’s an Olympic record time, too.

Ex-Olympic champion Adrian Moorhouse said on BBC TV: ‘The sprint Daniel Wiffen had at the end was incredible and Bobby Finke could do nothing about it.

‘His stroke rate in the last 50m was impressive, he was just charging for the wall.

‘Gregorio Paltrinieri did all he could but it wasn’t quite enough. What a wonderful swim, a masterclass of tactical swimming from Wiffen.’

They took the first set 6-3 with an early break – but Andy Murray and Dan Evans are being made to work a little harder in this second set.

6-3 2-2 is the current score against their Belgian second-round opponents.

Andy Murray and Dan Evans have taken the first set 6-3 against Belgium’s Sander Gille and Joran Vliegen in under 45 minutes!

The British duo only needed that singular early break in the end… They also managed to fend off three break points over the first set in total.

If they win this match, the Team GB pair will book their place in the quarter-finals.

That’s three games in a row for Andy Murray and Dan Evans!

They lead 3-0 against Sander Gille and Joran Vliegen in the first set of this second-round men’s doubles match at Roland-Garros.

This couldn’t be much more different to their opening victory, in which they made a very slow start before conjuring up a mind-bending comeback.

Andy Murray and Dan Evans lead 2-0 in the first set of their second-round men’s doubles match against Sander Gille and Joran Vliegen!

The British duo fended off two break points in the first game – and then shocked their opponents in game two to open up the lead.

This could possibly be Murray’s final match before retirement… let’s see if he can keep it going a little while longer.

ðð¾SO PROUD OF OUR GIRLSðð¾4ï¸â£th place in the Olympic team final with a total score of 164.263ðCongratulations Abi, Alice, Becky, Georgia-Mae and Ruby – you are 5 of  the greats! â¨This team has been excellent vibes throughout the Games and we are so  thrilled that theirâ¦pic.twitter.com/ymWwBlWMaA

On Team GB’s performance in the final, Smith added: ‘It is bittersweet, isn’t it? Team GB have come out into this team final and done every single routine clean.

‘There has not been one major mistake, a few wobbles but that is gymnastics. To come out here with the team that they have got, to do every routine clean and to be within two tenths of a bronze medal, it is harsh.

‘However they should be so, so proud of themselves.’

The moment United States and Simone Biles won the women’s team final ð¥ðºð¸#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/RmprnZC15J

It’s gold for Team USA and redemption for the brilliant Simone Biles!

They were the clear favourites going into the women’s team gymnastics final and performed like it, destroying their rivals and blowing away fans at a packed-out Bercy Arena.

Reacting to Biles’ performance, former British Olympian Louis Smith said: ‘We’ve just witnessed the best female gymnast in the world finish up her routine for what is the best team in the world at this Olympics. Bravo Simone Biles, bravo Team USA.’

There was not a huge amount of hope in Team GB getting a medal and they narrowly miss out on a place on the podium after a superb effort, finishing fourth.

Andy Murray will enter retirement from professional tennis after the Paris Olympics. The 37-year-old, who plays with a metal hip,confirmed ahead of the Gamesthat it would be his ‘last ever tennis tournament’.

That means thattonight’s second-round men’s doubles matchnext to his partner, Dan Evans, could officially bring the curtain down once and for all.

Murray and Evans conjured upone of the greatest comebacks of their careersin the first round and must now be feeling on top of the world.

The British duo lock horns with Sander Gille and Joran Vliegen, which is about to get underway on Court Suzanne-Lenglen at Roland-Garros.

The Belgians are experienced doubles players – and reached the French Open final last year – so they are tricky opponents on clay, which, worryingly, is very much a surface Murray hasn’t historically done very well on.

They lost the second set but Rafael Nadal and Carlos Alcaraz were on top throughout the tie-break decider, losing just two of the ten points.

Spain will take some beating in the men’s doubles…

Up next at Roland Garros: Andy Murray in potentially his final ever match.

The Scot teams up with Dan Evans and Great Britain face Joran Vliegen and Sander Gille of Belgium.

Simone Biles is showing her class in the team final but it’s not all been plain sailing for the American, who‘forgot’ Olympics protocol before the event.

Still, Team USA are looking good for gold in these early stages. Team GB were not expected to get a medal here but they’ve also made a decent start to proceedings.

Slay ð«Simone Biles makes the vault look easy in the women’s all-round team final.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/hIiS87n1gi

title: Olympics schedule and results: Swimming, dressage, gymnastics and tennis | Metro News
description: Latest news and live updates from 2024 Paris Olympic Games on day 4 (July 30) schedule: triathlon, swimming, dressage and gymnastics.
